heading: Preferences in making grants
label: 300d–42 Preferences in making grants
notes: Amendments2010— added subsecs. (a) to (c) and struck out former subsecs. (a) and (b) which related to preferences in making grants and preferences for certain applications.Pub. L. 111–148

amendments: Amendments2010— added subsecs. (a) to (c) and struck out former subsecs. (a) and (b) which related to preferences in making grants and preferences for certain applications.Pub. L. 111–148

Effective DateSection effective , with programs making awards providing financial assistance in fiscal year 1993 and subsequent years effective for awards made on or after , see section 801(b), (d)(1) of , set out as an Effective Date of 1992 Amendment note under .July 10, 1992Oct. 1, 1992Pub. L. 102–321section 236 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish an award basis for each eligible trauma center for grants under according to the percentage described in paragraph (2), subject to the requirements of .section 300d–41(a)(1) of this titlesection 300d–41(b)(3) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: With respect to a category A trauma center, 100 percent of the uncompensated care costs.

children.label: B
children.text: With respect to a category B trauma center, not more than 75 percent of the uncompensated care costs.

children.label: C
children.text: With respect to a category C trauma center, not more than 50 percent of the uncompensated care costs.

children.heading: In general1So in original. No par. (2) has been enacted.1
children.label: 1 In general1So in original. No par. (2) has been enacted.1
A
children.text: reserve 25 percent of the amount allocated for core mission awards for Level III and Level IV trauma centers; and

children.label: i
children.text: that have at least 1 graduate medical education fellowship in trauma or trauma related specialties for which demand is exceeding supply;

children.label: I
children.text: annual uncompensated care costs exceed $10,000,000; or

children.label: II
children.text: at least 20 percent of emergency department visits are charity or self-pay or Medicaid patients; and

children.label: iii
children.text: that are not eligible for substantial uncompensated care awards under .section 300d–41(a)(1) of this title

give preference to any application submitted by a trauma center that provides trauma care in a geographic area in which the availability of trauma care has significantly decreased or will significantly decrease if the center is forced to close or downgrade service or growth in demand for trauma services exceeds capacity; and
reallocate any emergency awards funds not obligated due to insufficient, or a lack of qualified, applications to the significant uncompensated care award program.
